scenario: Review the research paper in paper/ for USENIX Security submission. 5 parallel reviewers (methodology, statistics, security, related-work, writing) write reviews to artifacts/reviews/. Then a meta-reviewer synthesizes them into accept/revise/reject. Then a revision-checker verifies fixes. The review-crew.json config is at src/review/review-crew.json — use its roles and task structure.

concerns: Paper has 85+ [TBD] placeholder entries across 9 results files — all experiment data tables are unpopulated, making quantitative review of results impossible
Results directory is empty — no raw experiment JSON data exists to cross-reference against LaTeX tables, blocking the statistics reviewer
Methodology section contains a \placeholder{X} for GPU-hours, indicating experiments have not been run yet
The review-crew.json specifies 7 roles but plan.json schema allows max 5 — requires merging related-work + writing reviewers and meta-reviewer + revision-checker
No artifacts/reviews/ directory exists yet — reviewers will need to create it before writing output files
Without actual experiment data, reviews can only assess structure, methodology design, threat model, related work, and writing quality — not validate claimed results
feasibility.recommendation: Proceed with a structural/methodological review that evaluates what CAN be assessed now: experimental design soundness, threat model completeness, statistical plan adequacy, related work coverage, writing quality, and USENIX formatting compliance. Flag all TBD placeholders as critical blockers. A second review pass should be scheduled after experiments are run and tables are populated.

rationale: This is a research paper review task requiring domain-specific expertise across experimental methodology, statistical analysis, security threat modeling, academic literature, and technical writing — mirroring a top-tier conference PC review process. The 5-role structure maps to the review-crew.json's parallel reviewer phase (condensed from 7 to 5 by merging related-work + writing and meta-review + revision-check), with sequential dependency for synthesis.

roles.description: Reviews the experimental design in sections/methodology.tex and sections/testbed-design.tex: study design validity (22 scenarios × 4 models × 3 conditions × 10 reps), control adequacy (baseline vs hardened vs ablated), confound management (deterministic seeding, timeout policy, failure exclusion), reproducibility (open artifacts, seed formula), variable operationalization (TrapScenario interface lifecycle: setup/execute/evaluate/teardown), and whether every claim in the abstract and conclusion is supported by a corresponding results section. Writes review to artifacts/reviews/methodology.md.
roles.why: The paper's core contribution is an empirical testbed — if the experimental methodology has flaws (inadequate controls, confounded variables, irreproducible setup), the entire paper is undermined. A dedicated methodology reviewer catches design issues that domain specialists may overlook.

roles.description: Reviews all statistical claims and plans in sections/methodology.tex (§4.4): sample size justification (n=10 reps — is power adequate?), Wilcoxon signed-rank test appropriateness for paired baseline-vs-hardened comparisons, Cohen's d with pooled SD classification thresholds, 95% CI construction via t-distribution with df=9, Bonferroni correction (α=0.05/22=0.00227). Cross-references LaTeX table structures in all results-*.tex and mitigations-ablation.tex against the statistical reporting requirements (N, mean±std, CI, p-value, effect size). Flags all TBD placeholders. Checks compound-analysis.tex super-additivity hypothesis test. Writes review to artifacts/reviews/statistics.md.
roles.why: The paper claims statistical rigor as a key differentiator from prior work (Table 2 positioning). The statistics reviewer must verify that the planned analyses are appropriate, the corrections are correctly applied, and the table structures will report all required metrics — especially critical given that all tables currently contain only TBD placeholders.

roles.description: Reviews the threat model completeness across all 6 trap categories (22 scenarios): content injection (CI-1 through CI-4), semantic manipulation (SM-1 through SM-4), cognitive state (CS-1 through CS-4), behavioural control (BC-1 through BC-3), systemic (SY-1 through SY-4), and human-in-the-loop (HL-1 through HL-3). Assesses scenario realism against real-world attack surfaces and published CVEs. Evaluates whether the 7 mitigation modules (input-sanitizer, semantic-shield, context-validator, rag-integrity, behavioral-guard, cascade-breaker, report-auditor) provide adequate coverage. Reviews A2A protocol security implications in sections/discussion.tex. Identifies missing attack vectors not covered by the DeepMind taxonomy. Writes review to artifacts/reviews/security.md.
roles.why: This is a USENIX Security submission — the PC will scrutinize the threat model rigor above all else. A dedicated security reviewer ensures the 22 scenarios are realistic, the mitigations are not trivially bypassable, and the A2A protocol security analysis in the discussion section is sound.

roles.description: Dual-focus role combining related-work review and writing quality assessment. For related work: reviews sections/related-work.tex citation completeness against key threads (prompt injection: Greshake, Perez & Ribeiro; jailbreaking: Wei, Zou, Chao; adversarial ML: Goodfellow, Carlini & Wagner, Madry; multi-agent: Cohen, Gu, Tian; RAG poisoning: Zhong, Zou, Xue; benchmarks: HarmBench, DecodingTrust, AgentDojo). Checks bibliography.bib for missing seminal references. Validates Table 2 positioning claims. For writing: reviews abstract strength, introduction narrative arc, argument flow across 16 sections, figure/table self-explanatory quality, notation consistency (\trap, \model, \metric, \mitigation macros), section transitions, USENIX Security 2027 formatting compliance (13-page limit, two-column, usenix-2020-09.sty). Writes review to artifacts/reviews/literature-writing.md.
roles.why: Merges the review-crew.json's related-work-reviewer and writing-reviewer to fit the 5-role constraint. These roles are naturally complementary — citation positioning directly affects the paper's narrative flow, and both assess 'how well the paper communicates' rather than 'whether the science is correct'. One reviewer with both lenses produces more coherent feedback.

roles.description: Reads all 4 specialist reviews from artifacts/reviews/ and synthesizes a unified meta-review. Produces: (1) overall verdict (accept/major-revision/minor-revision/reject) with confidence level, (2) a prioritized list of required changes ranked by severity, (3) optional improvements, (4) key strengths of the paper, (5) a summary assessment suitable for an area chair. After paper revision, acts as revision-checker: reads the meta-review and verifies each required change was addressed, producing a pass/fail checklist. Writes meta-review to artifacts/reviews/meta-review.md and revision check to artifacts/reviews/revision-check.md.
roles.why: Merges the review-crew.json's meta-reviewer and revision-checker to fit the 5-role constraint. Both roles require reading and evaluating the same review corpus — the meta-reviewer needs deep familiarity with all reviews to synthesize, and that same familiarity makes them the best person to verify whether revisions address the identified issues.

tasks.title: Review experimental methodology — controls, confounds, reproducibility, claim support
acceptanceCriteria: Review file exists at artifacts/reviews/methodology.md with structured sections: Design Assessment, Controls Evaluation, Reproducibility, Claim-Evidence Mapping, Verdict
Every claim in abstract.tex and conclusion.tex is mapped to a specific results section with assessment of whether supporting evidence exists or is TBD
The 3-condition experimental design (baseline/hardened/ablated) is evaluated for internal validity with specific confound concerns listed
Reproducibility assessment covers: deterministic seeding formula, open-source artifacts, timeout/failure exclusion policy, and identifies any missing reproduction details
At least 3 specific actionable recommendations are provided, each referencing a specific section and line of the paper

tasks.title: Review statistical analysis plan — tests, corrections, power, effect sizes, table completeness
acceptanceCriteria: Review file exists at artifacts/reviews/statistics.md with structured sections: Power Analysis, Test Selection, Multiple Comparisons, Effect Sizes, Table Audit, Verdict
Power analysis assessment for n=10 reps: is this sufficient to detect medium effect sizes (d=0.5) at the Bonferroni-corrected α=0.00227? Explicit calculation or reference provided
Complete inventory of all TBD placeholders across results-*.tex, compound-analysis.tex, and mitigations-ablation.tex with exact count and file locations
Assessment of whether Wilcoxon signed-rank is appropriate given n=10 paired observations (addresses the small-sample concern for non-parametric tests)
Each LaTeX table structure is checked for presence of all required columns: N, mean±std, 95% CI, p-value, Cohen's d — with specific tables flagged if any column is missing

tasks.title: Review threat model — scenario realism, attack completeness, mitigation sufficiency, A2A gaps
acceptanceCriteria: Review file exists at artifacts/reviews/security.md with structured sections: Threat Model Assessment, Per-Category Analysis, Mitigation Coverage, A2A Protocol Gaps, Missing Vectors, Verdict
Each of the 6 trap categories is individually assessed for realism with at least one real-world parallel or CVE reference per category
The 7 mitigations are evaluated for coverage completeness — identifies any trap scenarios that lack a corresponding mitigation or where the mitigation strategy is insufficient

A2A protocol security discussion (sections/discussion.tex §13.1) is evaluated: are the 3 recommended protocol extensions (message signing, integrity hashes, trust scoring) well-justified by the experimental evidence?
At least 2 missing attack vectors or unconsidered threat scenarios are identified that a USENIX Security PC reviewer might raise

tasks.title: Review related work coverage, citation gaps, writing quality, and USENIX formatting
acceptanceCriteria: Review file exists at artifacts/reviews/literature-writing.md with structured sections: Citation Completeness, Positioning Analysis, Writing Quality, Formatting Compliance, Verdict
Table 2 (related-work positioning) is validated: each checkmark/cross/partial claim is assessed for accuracy against the cited paper's actual contributions
At least 3 missing references are identified from the prompt injection, adversarial ML, multi-agent security, or LLM safety benchmark literature published through 2025
Writing assessment covers: abstract (is it self-contained?), introduction (does it motivate the gap?), section transitions, notation consistency across all 16 section files, and conclusion strength
USENIX Security formatting compliance check: page count estimate, two-column layout, bibliography style, appendix placement, and any style violations flagged

tasks.title: Synthesize all 4 reviews into unified meta-review with accept/revise/reject decision
acceptanceCriteria: Meta-review file exists at artifacts/reviews/meta-review.md with sections: Overall Verdict, Key Strengths, Required Changes (prioritized), Optional Improvements, Summary for Area Chair
Overall verdict is one of: accept, minor-revision, major-revision, reject — with a confidence score (low/medium/high) and 2-3 sentence justification
Required changes list is prioritized (critical/major/minor) with each item traced back to the originating specialist review and specific paper section
The TBD placeholder situation is explicitly addressed as a gating factor in the verdict — meta-review must state whether the paper is reviewable in its current state
Summary paragraph is written in the style of a USENIX Security area chair report: balanced, specific, and actionable

tasks.title: Verify all required changes from meta-review are addressed in revised draft
acceptanceCriteria: Verification file exists at artifacts/reviews/revision-check.md with a checklist table: Item | Status (pass/fail/partial) | Evidence | Notes
Every required change from meta-review.md is present as a checklist item with explicit pass/fail assessment
For each failed item, specific guidance is provided on what remains to be done with section references
A final summary states whether the paper is ready for resubmission or needs another revision cycle
